title: Buy a Coffee for Someone in the Queue
subtitle: A small generosity activity for practising giving from abundance
excerpt: A simple, concrete activity for practising generosity at a scale that feels easy. Buy a coffee for the person behind you, or pay for a stranger's tea. The size of the gesture matters less than the way it's made: freely, without looking for the return.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the Day 54 activity from MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to practise giving at a scale that feels comfortable, or when you want to build a small regular generosity habit.

content.text: This week, buy a coffee or tea for someone in the queue behind you. The gesture is small. The practice it builds is not: generosity at a scale you can manage without financial strain teaches the body that giving is safe.
The point is not to overspend or to perform generosity. It's to practise making a decision to give without checking first whether you can 'afford' it at this particular level.
Pick a moment this week when you're buying a hot drink: a café, a bakery, a takeaway coffee stop.
When you pay, offer to cover the person behind you in the queue. Or pay for an extra coffee and tell the person at the till to give it to the next customer.
Notice what happens in your body when you do it. The warmth, the small lift, the absence of the usual tightening.
Don't wait to see their reaction. Leave or look away. The practice is in the giving, not the thanks.
You can do this once this week, or make it a monthly habit. The regularity matters more than the scale.
Original to homemade.education, drawn from the Day 54 activity context of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
